core_mechanism: an interactive delegation protocol in which a resource-limited party outsources a computation to an untrusted powerful executor while keeping it private, by masking each input with independent secret randomness (one-time-pad-style blinding) and then adaptively issuing step-by-step instructions conditioned on the executor's previously reported outcomes, so the executor performs the computation without learning it (optionally with a challenge-based check to detect a cheating executor)
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on the client preparing single states randomly chosen from a finite set (blinding) and then driving the server with single-step instructions that depend on previous measurement outcomes, keeping inputs, outputs, and computation perfectly private while the server does the heavy lifting.
